identity.meaning: Represents strength, determination, and competitive spirit

ownership_history: Franchise awarded to Sahara India Pariwar in 2010
Faced financial and administrative disputes with BCCI
Eventually withdrawn from IPL after 2013 season

history.full_text: Pune Warriors India (PWI) was a franchise cricket team that participated in the Indian Premier League (IPL) from 2011 to 2013. The team was owned by Sahara India Pariwar and was introduced as part of the IPL's expansion. Despite having several high-profile players, including Yuvraj Singh and Sourav Ganguly, PWI struggled to achieve consistent success on the field. The team faced numerous challenges, including poor team balance, inconsistent performances, and off-field administrative issues. In its debut season in 2011, Pune Warriors India showed some promise but failed to qualify for the playoffs. The following seasons saw a decline in performance, with the team finishing in the lower half of the table. Off the field, disputes between Sahara Group and the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) regarding franchise fees and governance led to instability. Eventually, Sahara withdrew the team from the IPL after the 2013 season, bringing an end to Pune Warriors India's brief and turbulent existence in the league.
timeline: 2010: Franchise awarded to Sahara India Pariwar
2013: Franchise withdrawn from IPL due to disputes

key_eras.description: Decline phase with poor results and instability

records: One of the lowest win percentages in IPL history
Short-lived franchise due to administrative disputes
Struggled to compete consistently against stronger teams

rivalries.details: Indirect connection as both represent Pune in IPL history
